gaps: AUTHENTICATED / LOGGED-IN CORE IS A TOTAL BLIND SPOT. The whole transactional spine a current student actually lives in — my.unimelb (the SIS: enrolment, results, fees, timetable), Canvas/LMS (canvas.lms.unimelb, catalog.lms.unimelb), and the 13 *.app.unimelb form microsites — sits behind a login wall the public scrape cannot enter. They appear ONLY as ~52 outbound destinations in cross-site-flow. Every 'wire X to my.unimelb / Careers Online' recommendation (6.7, 3.2) is asserted blind: we never see the post-login IA, its nav vocabulary, or whether it already does the linking. The register should add an explicit 'behind-auth scope boundary' section and flag that the find->act gap (6.7) is inferred from the public side only.

NO MOBILE / RESPONSIVE DIMENSION AT ALL. Eight dimensions were covered; mobile is absent. Viewport meta exists in the HTML (confirmed width=device-width on students pages) so a real responsive audit is runnable from the raw page.html, but none was done: no check of tap-target size, mobile nav patterns, horizontal-scroll/overflow, or whether the deep 9-11 level nav (1.5) collapses usably on a phone. For a student estate this is a first-class gap, not an optional extra.

NO PERFORMANCE / PAGE-WEIGHT DIMENSION. meta.json carries byteLength for every page (the students sample is 191-231KB of HTML alone) but pages-index.csv drops it, so no page-weight/render-cost cut was run. Heavy templated pages (the 240KB hub pages, MDHS card grids) and the handbook SPA's client-render cost directly affect students on mobile/limited data. A byteLength distribution per domain + a 'heaviest templates' list is a cheap, high-credibility addition the data already supports.

NO FEES / FINANCE / CENSUS-DATE JOURNEY despite it being the single highest-stakes current-student topic. There are 18 topic deep-dives but fees-finance exists only as a stub in topic-deepdives/_pagelists/fees-finance.md — never written up. Census dates, fee deadlines, payment, FEE-HELP/HECS, refunds and financial hardship are where errors cost students money, yet the register has no finding on the fees information architecture or its seams (students.unimelb finance section <-> my.unimelb payment <-> scholarships <-> feerefundform.unimelb).

NO SEARCH-FUNCTION EVALUATION even though search is the de-facto IA. SUMMARY establishes the estate's #1 hub is a handbook /search URL (5,529 inbound) and 13,435 links point INTO search/results pages — search IS how students navigate — but nobody assessed whether on-site search actually works: is there one search box or many (handbook/Funnelback/faculty), does a query for 'special consideration' or 'census date' surface the canonical page, is there federated search across the 20 domains? The register treats search-as-canonical as a problem to remove (2.5) without testing whether search is the only thing holding the fragmented estate together.

NO USER/CX VALIDATION — the project's own corrections doc (data-and-synthesis-issues.md, D6) already flags this: the single journey map covers only accept->plan->enrol->timetable; the costliest findings (alumni handoff, scholarships, HDR, Indigenous, reverse-funnel) have ZERO journey steps or experience scores. Every 'add a bridge / CTA' recommendation (3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.7) is structural-evidence-only with no evidence a student actually fails at that seam. The register should carry D6's caveat forward and label these findings 'structural evidence, not yet CX-validated.'

NO ANALYTICS / TRAFFIC PROXY. Every priority leans on link-counts as an importance proxy, but link-volume != student demand. No GA/search-log/most-visited-page proxy was used (none may be available, but that limit is never stated). Consequence: the register cannot say which of the 36 fixes touches pages students actually hit. The in_c= tracking params littered through the crawled URLs (visible in template-clones.csv) hint internal analytics exist and could be requested — worth naming as the single highest-value external data ask.

EQUITY / INDIGENOUS / ACCESSIBILITY-AS-LIVED-EXPERIENCE is thin. Murrup Barak (Indigenous), disability/equity registration, and low-SES (Access Melbourne) appear only incidentally (e.g. 2.9 lists an equity registration twin). D6 explicitly names Indigenous support as an uncovered journey. Given these cohorts are the ones least able to absorb a fragmented estate, a dedicated equity-journey finding is missing.

INTERNATIONAL-STUDENT COMPLIANCE JOURNEY is under-developed relative to its risk. The visa-status orphan is noted (2.9) but there is no end-to-end view of the CoE / visa-condition / OSHC / enrolment-load compliance chain that, if a student misses it, has immigration consequences. This is the highest-consequence current-student journey and deserves its own finding, not a single orphan line.

ALT-TEXT / CONTACT-CHANNEL / 'who do I ask' SERVICE GAP. The register covers Stop 1 routing well but never maps the contacts-support estate as a whole (there IS a contacts-support.md deep-dive): is there one canonical 'contact us / ask a question' path, or N faculty variants + ask.unimelb + Stop 1 + my.unimelb enquiry? The 'how does a confused student reach a human' question is adjacent to 3.5 but not answered.

GOVERNANCE RECOMMENDATIONS LACK A CURRENT-STATE OWNERSHIP MAP. 5.1-5.6 propose registries and a parent template but never document who owns what today (which team runs students.unimelb vs the faculty Squiz instances vs the handbook vs the *.app forms). Without the as-is RACI the 'assign an owner' actions have no addressee. The existing service-model-matrix.md is the seed but isn't surfaced in the register.

CONTENT CURRENCY / FRESHNESS not assessed. meta.json has crawledAt but nothing checks last-modified or stale-content signals (e.g. pages still referencing prior years, the 'sandbox/2026-uplifts' and '...2'-suffixed pages hint at uncontrolled drafts/duplicates in production). A 'stale and draft content leaking to production' finding is supportable and missing.
verify_next: RUN LIVE HTTP STATUS PROBES before any redirect/404 claim ships. CRITICAL: nothing in the project ever issued an HTTP request — grep for requests.get/curl -I/status_code across analysis/ and scratchpad/ returns empty. Every '301/404/502/phantom-404' claim (2.1, 2.3, 2.4, 2.8, 4.6, 5.4, and the headline '?a= manufactures 3,359 phantom 404s') is URL-STRING INFERENCE from crawled page titles, not real response codes. HEAD-probe the ~32 ?a= twins, the 419 'dead' destinations, the 28 www-vs-bare pairs, and a sample of the 120 parallel-tree leaves to confirm which truly 404/301/200 today. This is the single biggest evidence weakness in the register.

MAKE THE NEAR-DUPLICATE DETECTION REPRODUCIBLE. Findings 4.1 (33 HDR pages Jaccard=1.000), 4.2 (59 FBE/MBS career pages), 4.3 (7 'Employability in X') all cite shingle-Jaccard scores, but NO jaccard/shingle/minhash script exists in the repo (grep matched only the literal word inside a markdown doc) and fingerprint-based dup found exactly 0. The only saved dup artifact is template-clones.csv, which is TITLE-normalisation, not body similarity. Write and commit the shingle-Jaccard script, regenerate a near-dup CSV, and confirm the 33/59/7 counts and the =1.000 scores before these 'redirect the mirrors' actions reach a decision.

RECONCILE THE *.app HOST COUNT. Register 5.5 says '19 single-purpose transaction microsites (13 on *.app)'; cross-site-flow yields exactly 13 distinct *.app.unimelb destination hosts and the '6 standalone form hosts' need re-listing from the data. Re-derive the authoritative forms-host list from cross-site-flow + domain-edges and correct the headline number (it currently mixes app + non-app and double-states '19').

RECONCILE THE students.unimelb PAGE COUNT discrepancy carried INSIDE the evidence pack. nav-depth.csv and index.json both say 833, but SUMMARY.md's own orphan table says 679 for students.unimelb. The register asserts 833 (correcting the deck's 74) — good — but should explain/fix the 679-vs-833 split in SUMMARY before citing it, or a reviewer will catch the internal contradiction.

VERIFY THE '120 parallel-tree leaves' precisely. Confirmed directionally (index.json shows BOTH /course-admin=212 and /your-course=101, /support-and-wellbeing=152 and /student-support=56 as live roots), but the exact '120 leaf-names under 2+ roots / 75+24' figure in 1.1 needs the leaf-overlap script committed and its output saved, since it's the #1 'do this FIRST' action and the acceptance test ('target 0 leaves under 2+ roots') depends on the precise number.

CONFIRM THE HANDBOOK INCAPSULA ARTIFACT QUANTUM with a JS-enabled re-crawl. The register both uses (2.2, 6.6) and disowns (5.7 caveat) the handbook orphan/thin numbers, citing '~1,470 of 2,134 are 887-byte Incapsula shells.' a11y_verify.py already detects the shell ('Request unsuccessful' / <1500 bytes) — re-run it to lock the exact shell count, then re-crawl handbook with a politeness delay so the 266-orphan / 14%-orphaned figure is either confirmed or retired, not left in both states.

VERIFY THE STAFF-INTRANET LEAK IS STUDENT-FACING. 5.6 cites eng->staff.unimelb=838, msd->729 (confirmed in cross-site-flow). But confirm these originate on CURRENT-STUDENT pages, not staff/about pages within the faculty domain — sample 10 of the referring URLs. If the 838 are mostly footer/staff-section links the 'students routed to staff system' framing weakens; if they're on /students pages it strengthens. Cheap to check, changes the severity.

PRESSURE-TEST THE 'no contextual handoff' claims (alumni 97, careers 102) — both verified to the exact number against cross-site-flow, so the COUNTS are solid; what's unverified is the INTERPRETATION that these are all event-RSVP/nav noise. Sample the actual 97 alumni and 102 careers anchor contexts to confirm none is a genuine lifecycle handoff before asserting 'zero in-content bridge' to stakeholders.

VALIDATE THE SCHOLARSHIP DUPLICATE-TITLE AND ALT-TEXT NUMBERS against the committed scripts. 6.2 (2,038/2,151 identical <title>) and 6.4 (4,346/4,346 listing imgs missing alt) come from verify_titles_handbook.py / verify_blank_scholimg.py / verify_alt_and_study.py which DO exist in scratchpad — re-run them on the full (not 250-page) sample and confirm the percentages hold estate-wide before they ship as quick-wins, since 6.4 extrapolates from a 250-page sample to 'hundreds of listing URLs.'

CHECK WHETHER my.unimelb / Canvas ALREADY PROVIDE the links 6.7 and 3.2 say are missing. Because the authenticated side is uncrawlable, request screenshots or a walkthrough of the logged-in handbook 'enrol' flow and Careers Online before asserting the find->act gap. If my.unimelb already deep-links enrolment from a subject code, 6.7's premise ('catalogue is a read-only dead-end') is only true for the public handbook, which changes the fix from 'add enrol action' to 'expose the existing authed action publicly.'
verdict: Strong and well-evidenced on the dimensions it covers (the link-graph seam, IA, duplication, governance and a11y findings verify cleanly against the data), but NOT yet complete: it is blind to the authenticated transactional core (my.unimelb/Canvas/forms), omits mobile, performance, fees/finance, search-function, equity and CX-validation entirely, and — most importantly — its entire redirect/404 remediation layer rests on URL-string inference with zero live HTTP probing, while its headline duplication findings (4.1/4.2/4.3) ride on an ad-hoc Jaccard analysis that was never saved as a reproducible script.
